assumptions: The exposure relates to a foreign-currency-denominated intragroup dividend receivable recognised by one group entity against another.
The question concerns the accounting in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk affecting profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a separate hedge accounting model, including for foreign currency risk on highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 treat a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model, relevant where intragroup monetary items form part of the net investment.

approaches.label: IAS 21 foreign currency accounting without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the baseline accounting for exchange differences on monetary items in profit or loss, except where a qualifying hedge accounting model changes that treatment.
